files_to_change.change: Add unexported maxNodeIDInStore helper; call it from NewNodeAllocator to cross-check restored high-water-mark against nodes actually present on disk; return descriptive error on mismatch.
Append TestNodeAllocatorCrossChecksExistingNodes (new, own doc-comment header, appended at end of file).

files_not_touched: engine/btree/btree_test.go (owned by issue #41's bc08c0a and future 4.5.12.6)

callers_affected.callers: engine/btree package-internal only (Tree constructors / test scaffolding); no engine/split, engine/rpc, or api/ callers construct NodeAllocator directly per grep
callers_affected.behavior_change: NewNodeAllocator now returns a non-nil error in the new stale-sidecar-vs-nodes-present case; previously succeeded (incorrectly) in that case. All existing passing call sites (fresh sidecar co-created with a fresh/consistent index file) are unaffected since maxPresent <= next always held for them already.

concurrency_scope_check.note: Per dispatcher's concurrency note: other agents this session work engine/rpc, api/main.go, agents/query, docs/LLD/*.md, engine/wal -- none overlap engine/btree/*.go. Confirmed via git status: no other uncommitted engine/btree changes present at run start.

regression_risk_closed.summary: NodeAllocator sidecar-loss/ID-reuse residual risk, explicitly recommended to be closed before persist/reload subtask locks in on-disk contract; this subtask closes it.
